danger_signals.meaning: Enemy Terminator units and Skynet machines represent immediate threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Dystopian industrial landscapes (based on film source material)
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile world-state where humanity is hunted by machines
Populated areas under siege; civilian danger zones

safety_signals.meaning: Human resistance fighter; represents survival and human agency against machines

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Terminator units (implied from platformer enemy design)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Future industrial wastelands (2029 setting); dystopian architecture
Los Angeles 1984 urban locations (police station, nightclub); represents pre-Judgment Day human civilization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Industrial dystopian level design; machine-dominated environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kyle Reese battles Skynet machines; survival platforming mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban police station and nightclub settings; civilian infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Evading terminator threat within populated human spaces; protection objectives

environmental_storytelling.example: Game juxtaposes 2029 future war levels against 1984 Los Angeles locations to communicate timeline conflict and stakes
Future war levels present industrial hazards; urban levels present civilian infrastructure as navigation obstacles
Vehicular sections communicate escape/chase scenarios; urgency of survival against pursuing machines

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Platforming navigation, combat against machines, protection of human civilians
Hunt player character; represent persistent threat across multiple world-states

notes: Genesis version (1992, Probe Software/Virgin Games) is a shoot 'em up variant. Wikipedia source provides limited visual/manifestation detail for Genesis port specifically. Data derived primarily from game structure (platformer with shoot 'em up elements, Kyle Reese protagonist, level design spanning 2029 future and 1984 LA) and film source material context. Actual visual assets and specific danger/safety signaling mechanics not documented in available source.
